title: Environmental Sensor Battery Status
spl:
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:battery_status"
| eval hours_since_report=round((now()-last_report_time)/3600,1)
| where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24
| table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
| sort battery_level_pct
description: Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.
value: Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.
implementation: Poll sensor management interface (SNMP, API) for battery_level_pct and last_report_time. Poll daily or every 6 hours. Alert on battery <20% or no report in 24+ hours (possible dead battery). Maintain sensor inventory with battery replacement schedule. Report on sensors due for battery change.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP modular input, custom (sensor API).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Sensor management interface (battery_level_pct, last_report_time).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll sensor management interface (SNMP, API) for battery_level_pct and last_report_time. Poll daily or every 6 hours. Alert on battery <20% or no report in 24+ hours (possible dead battery). Maintain sensor inventory with battery replacement schedule. Report on sensors due for battery change.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:battery_status"
| eval hours_since_report=round((now()-last_report_time)/3600,1)
| where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24
| table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
| sort battery_level_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Environmental Sensor Battery Status** ‚Äî Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.

Documented **Data sources**: Sensor management interface (battery_level_pct, last_report_time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP modular input, custom (sensor API). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: environment; **sourcetype**: sensor:battery_status. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=environment, sourcetype="sensor:battery_status". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since_report** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Environmental Sensor Battery Status**): table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (low battery sensors), Gauge (lowest battery %), Bar chart (sensors by battery level), Single value (sensors needing battery replacement).
visualization: Table (low battery sensors), Gauge (lowest battery %), Bar chart (sensors by battery level), Single value (sensors needing battery replacement).
